title: Empathy boosts the comprehension of nonverbal behavior.

assets.abstract: Empathy is a fundamental component of social cognition, yet its influence on the neural processing of non-verbal cues in neurotypical populations remains insufficiently explored. Using EEG/ERPs, we examined empathy-dependent modulation of gesture recognition during a caption - gesture congruence task, manipulating face visibility in participants with high versus low Empathy Quotients (EQ). Participants, classified via two standardized empathy scales, viewed 800 gesture images preceded by short t

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Social Incarnation — intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing, second-person neuroscience.
